content.rendered:
Stevie Wonder’s “Signed, Sealed, Delivered I’m Yours” was a smash hit from the beginning, topping the U.S. R&B chart for six weeks and peaking at #3 on the Hot 100 in 1970.

The song signaled an inflection point in Wonder’s career on the wings of a new creative confidence and artistic control over his work.

But “Signed, Sealed, Delivered I’m Yours” earned a more permanent place in history in 2008, when U.S. presidential candidate (and Bruce’s future BFF) Barack Obama embraced it as his campaign’s theme song all the way through Election Day and his first on-stage appearance in Grant Park as president-elect.

The Obama campaign played Wonder’s 1970 single at virtually every rally that election season, so when Bruce Springsteen, Billy Joel, John Legend, and India Arie banded together in support of Obama during the final push, there was really only one song they could have chosen to end the night on.

Bruce and Billy’s main set has been circulating broadly for a while now, but the encores are a bit harder to come by. So take a listen and enjoy the exuberant finale of the Change Rocks! benefit from October 16, 2008, featuring Legend and Arie on lead vocals, with support from Bruce on guitar and Billy on piano.

And if audio from that performance is rare, video is scarcer still. But check this out: it’s only about a minute or so, but it’s enough to capture the energy and excitement in the room that night, and the anticipation of eight years to come.

Signed, Sealed, Delivered I’m Yours
First performed: October 16, 2008 (New York City, NY)
Last performed: October 16, 2008 (New York City, NY)

© November 29, 2021

excerpt.rendered: One time only: John Legend, India Arie, Billy Joel, and Bruce Springsteen join forces on an anticipatory, celebratory cover of Stevie Wonder’s 1970 classic.

jetpack-related-posts.title: October 16
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: On this date: Bruce debuts "Long Time Comin'" nine years before its album release, a star-studded show at the Roxy, Bruce and Billy Joel perform for Barack Obama, and more.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Roll of the Dice: Forward!
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Be careful what you wish for when you ask Bruce Springsteen to write your campaign song.

jetpack-related-posts.title: River Deep – Mountain High
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Watch Bruce back Tina Turner one of her signature songs, then go vote her in to the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame!

jetpack-related-posts.title: Roll of the Dice: Working on a Dream
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: In defense of "Working on a Dream," the perfect title track for one of Bruce's best and most underrated albums.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Blogger’s Note: New Music and Other Updates
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: For a summer without live music, it's been a remarkably busy time for Bruce Springsteen. Here's a quick round-up of the most must-watch/listen events of the summer.

jetpack-related-posts.title: We Are the World
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce was just one of many who participated in the USA For Africa project, but his contributions provided some of the song's most memorable moments.
